prevalence.trend: stable to increasing in urban areas due to pollution and allergen exposure

burden.mortalityRate: Low with controller access; severe exacerbations can be fatal

vulnerablePopulations: children with poor access to inhaled corticosteroids
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Recurrent wheeze/cough with variability; spirometry in older children when possible; symptom patterns and response to bronchodilator/controller therapy; exclude alternative diagnoses in preschool wheeze

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; case-based diagnosis in APS
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; depends on inhaler access, education, and adherence

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Spirometry access limited outside urban centers; diagnosis often clinical
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: ICS as controller; SABA for relief; action plans and trigger avoidance; severe asthma referral for biologics in specialized centers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Inhaler availability and adherence, pollution exposure, and ED overload during exacerbation seasons
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Primary care network and availability of basic inhalers in SUS in many areas
